description: The Staff Selection Commission (HQ) has announced the first round of tentative allocation (FRTA) for Combined Graduate Level (CGL) Examination, 2025. Cut-offs vary by category, and 107 results are withheld. A sliding mechanism will run from April 13–22, 2026, for vacancy reallocation. Final answer keys and detailed marks will be released on the SSC website.

The conflict, initiated by coordinated strikes on Iranian leadership, expanded to disrupt global energy markets and shipping routes. President Trump's objectives and timelines shifted throughout the war, culminating in a fragile pause.

His century of sixes in the IPL is a remarkable feat, underscoring his destructive batting prowess and consistent performance for the franchise.

title: Hidden charges? Gulf conflict may raise LPG prices, but not your restaurant bill, says CCPA
description: The Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) has declared additional restaurant charges like "LPG charges" or "fuel surcharge" unlawful, reinforcing price transparency. These operational costs must be factored into menu prices, not added later without explicit consumer consent. This intervention aims to dismantle unfair trade practices and restore trust in everyday transactions.

Voters can use their EPIC number or personal details. Registered mobile numbers also facilitate verification. Downloading the complete voter list for polling booths is also possible. This advance check helps streamline the voting process.

description: Aditya Dhar’s espionage epic, 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge,' continues to script history at the global box office. On day 21, Wednesday, April 8, 2026, the Ranveer Singh-led thriller solidified its legendary status by becoming the first Hindi film ever to surpass the Rs. 1,000 crore India net milestone. Having already outpaced giants like 'Jawan' and 'KGF: Chapter 2', the film has now amassed a domestic net of Rs. 1,034.00 crore. Despite the ongoing IPL 2026 season, the film remains a powerhouse, eyeing a Rs. 1,700 crore worldwide gross target.

The apology follows sharp criticism from the BJP, which deemed his statements insulting and unacceptable, highlighting Gujarat's literacy and contributions.

description: A boat decorator was arrested in Varanasi after a video of him drinking alcohol on the Ganga river surfaced online. This incident follows a previous viral video showing an iftar party's waste allegedly dumped into the river. Samajwadi Party president Akhilesh Yadav criticized the ruling BJP, linking increased alcohol consumption to citizen stress.

description: North Korea fired multiple ballistic missiles on Wednesday. This followed a rare conciliatory message from leader Kim Jong Un to South Korean President Lee Jae Myung. Seoul convened an emergency meeting and urged Pyongyang to stop violating UN resolutions. Analysts suggest North Korea is asserting strength while seeking nuclear power recognition.

description: Director Atlee expressed deep emotion following the reveal of Allu Arjun's 'Raaka' first look, calling it an 18-year-old dream. The poster, released on Arjun's birthday, has generated significant buzz, hinting at a rugged action thriller with a wild, tribal tone. With Deepika Padukone also confirmed, expectations are soaring for this highly anticipated project.

The veteran wicketkeeper-batter is undergoing rehabilitation for a calf muscle strain and is unlikely to be match-ready soon. His absence coincides with CSK's poor start, having lost their first three matches, highlighting a critical gap in crunch situations.

description: The Election Commission of India has assured that the 2026 West Bengal Assembly elections will be conducted without fear, violence, or intimidation. This statement follows a meeting with the TMC, which accused the ECI of bias towards the BJP and alleged systematic disenfranchisement of voters through electoral roll revisions.

title: First in 7 years! India to get Iran crude oil on US sanctions waiver
description: India, the world’s third-largest importer and consumer of oil, has not taken any shipments from Iran since May 2019, when US sanctions led it to halt such purchases.

This stance contrasts with Iran's broader peace proposal, highlighting ongoing regional complexities and differing interpretations of the truce.

Stefon Diggs controversy grows as Cardi B fears for safety amid chaos
description: Cardi B has raised serious safety concerns, saying she feels her life is in danger amid ongoing online harassment linked to Stefon Diggs. The situation escalated after viral rumors and legal debates surfaced. Meanwhile, Offset was shot near a Florida casino but is recovering. With Diggs also facing legal issues, the controversy has intensified.

title: UP districts bordering dry Bihar ‘high’ on liquor revenue; alcohol flows via river routes
description: Districts bordering Bihar are leading Uttar Pradesh in excise revenue, with some exceeding targets by over 100%. This surge is attributed to tipplers from the 'dry' state crossing over or engaging in bulk purchases for smuggling. Even economically weaker eastern UP districts are outperforming affluent western counterparts, highlighting a significant cross-border demand.

title: Canada has changed immigration rules; changing visa fee, work permit rules and more
description: Canada's immigration landscape is shifting in April 2026 with increased passport and citizenship fees, offset by a 30-day processing guarantee. Super visa rules are relaxed, allowing sponsors more flexibility. Provinces gain more control over candidate assessment, and rural employers can hire more foreign workers. Saskatchewan introduces new application fees for its nominee program.
